landing.title: ikimon.life — Enjoy Life through nearby nature
landing.heroHeadingPlain: Your usual walk becomes an adventure.
landing.heroLead: Walk, find something, and save it with a place. When names and differences become a little clearer, you want to walk again.
heroPromiseChips: Nearby nature gets a little more interesting
One travel photo becomes a reason to return

dailyDashboard.title: Choose a reason to go outside
dailyDashboard.lead: Record, revisit, or look nearby. The first screen stays focused on enjoying nature today.
dailyDashboard.seasonalEmpty: Seasonal clues are still being collected

recordToday.title: Save today's find
recordToday.body: Save what you found with a photo and a short note.

revisitPlace.title: Go back to a place you walked
revisitPlace.body: The same place changes when the season moves.

nearbyPulse.title: See where nearby records are moving
nearbyPulse.body: Use active areas as a reason for the next walk.

needsId.title: Open a record that needs a name
needsId.body: Turn an uncertain record into the next discovery.

landing.toolSectionTitle: Look on site. Choose from the map.
landing.toolSectionLead: Check what caught your eye, or choose where to walk next. Both paths lead back to a record you can revisit.

lens.title: Look closer at what caught your eye
lens.body: Point the camera and AI returns candidates and clues. It helps you keep the moment without pretending to be the final answer.

scan.title: Choose today's walk from the map
scan.body: Use nearby finds, seasonal changes, and gaps to choose the next place worth walking.

landing.mapSectionTitle: Find places that make you want to walk again
landing.mapSectionLead: Nearby paths and travel stops both hold reasons for the next walk. See what has been found and decide one next step.
landing.mapEmpty: No observations on the map yet

fieldLoop.title: Find. Learn. Save. Help someone else.
fieldLoop.lead: Find something, check a few clues, and save it with the place. The more people keep these small records, the easier nearby nature becomes to understand.

steps.title: 1. Find
steps.body: Notice something on a walk or while traveling.

steps.title: 2. Learn
steps.body: Use AI clues and your own eyes to look a little closer.

steps.title: 3. Save
steps.body: Keep the photo, place, time, and a short note together.

steps.title: 4. Help
steps.body: Small records can become clues for local nature, research, and conservation.

principles: Keep records even before you know the name.
Turn each record into the next walk.

community.title: Community | ikimon
community.heading: Join walks and themed surveys
community.lead: Start with a solo walk, then sometimes look at the same place with others. Community events, themed surveys, and small local programs live here.
